title: Can I Retire at 35 With $500k? A Lean FIRE Reality Check
meta_title: Can I Retire at 35 With $500k? Lean FIRE & NRI Math
subtitle: Every strategy simulated — withdrawal plans, real estate, dividends, and the geo-arbitrage path an NRI actually used to FIRE in Southeast Asia. Just math.
description: Can you retire at 35 with $500k (~₹4.7 crore)? Every strategy run with real math — withdrawal rates, dividends, real estate, and the geo-arbitrage route an NRI used to FIRE in Southeast Asia.

faqs.question: Can I retire at 35 with $500k in the United States?
faqs.answer: For most people, retiring at 35 with $500k in the United States is not realistic because the portfolio must support 50 or more years of expenses, healthcare, taxes, and inflation.

faqs.question: How much income does $500k produce with the 4% rule?
faqs.answer: A $500k portfolio produces about $20,000 per year, or $1,667 per month, using a 4% withdrawal rate before taxes.

faqs.question: Can geo-arbitrage make retiring at 35 with $500k possible?
faqs.answer: Geo-arbitrage can make a $500k retirement more realistic if annual expenses fall low enough, especially in lower-cost countries or very low-cost domestic locations.

faqs.question: Can an NRI retire at 35 with $500k in Southeast Asia?
faqs.answer: Yes. For an NRI, keeping about $500k (~₹4.7 crore) invested in USD and living in Southeast Asia on roughly $1,100–$1,800 a month is one of the cleanest early-retirement paths. At a conservative 3% withdrawal it has a 96%+ chance of lasting 55 years, and the portfolio typically keeps growing in real terms.

faqs.question: Is ₹4–5 crore enough to retire early abroad?
faqs.answer: Roughly ₹4–5 crore (about $500k) is not enough to retire at 35 in a US city, but it comfortably funds early retirement in Southeast Asia — Vietnam, Thailand and similar — where a full lifestyle costs around ₹1–1.7 lakh a month. Geography, not the amount alone, decides whether it works.
